description_long: This sculpture depicts a robed female figure, likely representing the Virgin Mary, standing on a rectangular plinth. The figure's head is bowed slightly, with a serene expression and hands extended gently downward and outward in a gesture of blessing or welcome. The drapery of the robe is rendered with soft, flowing folds, and the overall surface has a weathered, rust-brown patina, suggesting it is made of cast metal, possibly iron. The back of the sculpture shows the robe cascading in heavy folds, and the base is simple and unadorned. The figure's features are stylized but recognizable, with a hooded cloak covering the head and shoulders. The sculpture has a solid, monolithic appearance and is likely intended for outdoor display.
description: A weathered, rust-brown metal sculpture of a robed female figure, likely the Virgin Mary, standing on a rectangular plinth with hands extended in a gesture of blessing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the serene face, outstretched hands, and detailed folds of the robe.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the drapery's depth, and the thickness of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the cascading folds of the robe and the smooth, rounded back of the head.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the profile and the way the robe falls along the body.
attribute_subject_matter: robed female figure, likely the Virgin Mary
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
